score: 0/0 high-priority agent capabilities affected by this diff
summary: dyad.berlin has no agent tool layer at all — no tool definitions, no system prompt construction, no LLM SDK dependency. Every user-facing action is an orphan feature relative to agents. However, this diff is a read-path bug fix only (body rendering pipeline for /conversations/[id]) and introduces no new user-facing actions. The fix cannot worsen agent-native parity because parity does not yet exist. The one relevant observation for this diff is that GET /api/prompts/[id] already returns the full PromptDetail object including body_html, and this PR ensures body_html is now always populated with full content rather than silently empty — which is a net improvement for any future agent consuming that endpoint.

observations.title: No agent tool layer exists in this codebase
observations.description: There are no tool definitions, system prompts, or LLM SDK dependencies anywhere in the source tree. Every user action (create/edit/publish prompt, respond, invite, give feedback) is accessible only through the browser UI. This is a pre-existing baseline gap, not introduced by this diff. Flagged here for visibility; the diff itself is neutral on this point.

observations.title: body_html in GET /api/prompts/[id] response is now reliable for API consumers
observations.description: Before this fix, GET /api/prompts/[id] could return an empty body_html string for prompts with content (due to the duplicate-Link extension bug and the narrow \n\n+ split in the IIFE fallback). Any future agent reading that endpoint would have received an empty field and had no way to read the conversation body. This diff corrects that: renderBodyHtmlOrFallback guarantees non-empty HTML whenever body contains text. The raw body (TipTap JSONContent) is also present in the response as the body field, giving a future agent two options for reading body content.

notes: The plan doc (docs/plans/2026-04-29-fix-truncated-conversation-body-plan.md) correctly scopes this as read-path only. No new UI actions, no new agent capabilities needed. Unit 3 (upstream root-cause patch) is still open; if it modifies validate-tiptap-content.ts or tiptap-html.ts the XSS posture should be re-checked but agent-native parity remains unaffected.
